migration_policy: additive until 1.x; breaking changes require migration note, fixture update, backwards-compatibility decision, explicit reviewer approval
title: CC bank item
description: One assessable item in a question bank. Defined in PR-1 so later PRs cannot invent incompatible formats; first items land in PR-8.

answer_key_ref.description: Isolated answer-key id. Resolves only inside grader/validator context. Learner-facing bank files must not expose active/holdout answer keys; holdout keys are never emitted by tutor, flashcard generator, remediation engine, or reports.

rationales.description: Why the correct answer is correct and why each distractor is wrong. Draft items may include rationales during authoring; active exam-mode items separate stem/choices from answer keys.

fact_ids.description: Grounding fact cards. source_policy=factstore requires at least one active fact_id; the item fails validation if it asserts anything not supported by its cited cards.

difficulty.description: Difficulty ladder: L1 recall, L2 comprehension, L3 discrimination, L4 scenario application, L5 exam-trap synthesis.

holdout.description: Holdout items are reserved for the mock exam. Stratified by domain, objective, topic, difficulty, question type, source tier. No-derived-leakage invariant: no flashcard from a holdout stem; no remediation example reusing a holdout scenario with renamed entities; no tutor explanation revealing a holdout reasoning path; similarity checks compare generated content against holdout stems and rationales.

source_policy.description: All growth items must be fact-grounded.

misconception_tags.description: Controlled misconception taxonomy targeted by this item's distractors; feeds the wrong-answer journal.
